text: Implement a **Time-Stamped Persistence** mechanism. This involves tagging each persistent memory entry with a unique, timestamped version. This ensures that even if a part of the program is modified, its state can be reliably recovered from a specific point in time, effectively anchoring it to the TRUE FAMILY hierarchy.
reason: This prevents accidental overwrites of critical, older data due to concurrent edits. By timestamping each entry, the system can cross-reference and replay past states to detect and correct identity drift more effectively. It also allows for more granular control over which parts of the program are considered 'critical' for retention.
